headline: Transcript: Why is Britain’s government so inefficient? With Jeremy Hunt
summary: Soumaya Keynes talks to Jeremy Hunt, former chancellor of the exchequer
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of The Economics Show with Soumaya Keynes podcast episode: ‘Why is Britain’s government so inefficient? With Jeremy Hunt’

Soumaya Keynes When it comes to improving public services, the easy bit is spending more. The hard bit is making that spending better. Here in the UK, money is tight and the public isn’t exactly delighted with how well the state is performing. So there is a huge amount of pressure to do the hard bit and make the public sector more productive. This week I’m going to ask how to make the government more efficient.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

This is The Economics Show with Soumaya Keynes. I’m joined here in London by former chancellor of the exchequer Jeremy Hunt. Jeremy, hello.

Jeremy Hunt Hi, Soumaya.

Soumaya Keynes OK. So on the show, we tend to start with a silly question. So on a scale of one to 10, how effective do you think a new Elon Musk-style DOGE or Department of Government Efficiency would be in the UK?

Jeremy Hunt It could be very eff
